evidence: "I do not know if these three Agatha Christie movies had the same director/producer but they felt like they came from the same makers."
"Yes, it's about murder but it's so tastefully done! No blood, no bodies, no autopsies--just lots of great dialogue between McMillan, Wife and Maid."
"It's worth watching and entertaining, just not something I'd bring with me to a desert island."
"Not great writing, acting or locations but still fun!"
"The shows were actually about Christmas and the writers wrote dialogue where the characters cared about each other but not in an overly sentimental kind of way."
"This was a great show, although I didn't appreciate it at the time."
"It's done without violence, blood or gore. Instead The Haunting relies on perfect direction, brilliant writing and amazing acting."
"Well written, acted, directed--McMillan and Wife was the best spoke on the NBC Mystery Wheel."
"I wanted to be Sally McMillan when I was 12 and planned to wear a football jersey and hoop earrings to bed when I married someone like Stuart McMillan."
"I could watch this show over and over and over and I probably will!"
"This is truly the scariest movie I have ever seen and it's done without violence, blood or gore."
"It's like eating at your favorite fast food place--predictable, enjoyable and satisfying."
"Neil Simon made heartbreaking material so entertaining."
"I loved the scene where George explained to Valerie why he slept with her."

summary: The user enjoys classic mystery and comedy genres, particularly those with strong writing and character development. They appreciate high production quality and emotional depth in storytelling, often favoring slower-paced narratives that evoke nostalgia and warmth.
